jokes.text: Michael's vague illness symptoms and Dwight's medical diagnosis attempts
jokes.setup: Michael claims to be dying with vague symptoms
jokes.punchline: Dwight takes it seriously and tries to diagnose him with medical terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - only these two would have this dynamic. Michael's hypochondria meets Dwight's earnest medical concern.

jokes.text: About 40 times a year, Michael gets really sick, but has no symptoms. Dwight is always gravely concerned.
jokes.setup: We just saw Michael's mysterious illness
jokes.punchline: Jim's matter-of-fact explanation reveals this is a regular occurrence
jokes.explanation: Efficient setup that recontextualizes the previous scene perfectly. Jim's deadpan delivery is key.

jokes.text: Ooh, is it possible you ate food that contained animal waste? It's possible.
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to diagnose Michael's fake illness
jokes.punchline: Michael casually agrees he might have eaten animal waste
jokes.explanation: Absurd medical question meets Michael's willingness to agree to anything dramatic.

jokes.text: To what do I owe this great honor, David Wallace? And Gromit.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to sound formal with his boss
jokes.punchline: Adds 'And Gromit' referencing Wallace and Gromit
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael random pop culture reference that doesn't quite work but fits his character.

jokes.text: Oh, you sigh like Jan.
jokes.setup: David Wallace sighs during the phone call
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately compares it to Jan, revealing his relationship baggage
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Michael's inability to separate personal and professional. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: I broke Jan's heart, David, and I feel awful. But you know what? Sometimes you just gots to get your freak on.
jokes.setup: Michael expressing genuine remorse about Jan
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermines it with 'gots to get your freak on'
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael - genuine emotion immediately undercut by inappropriate phrasing. The whiplash is perfect.

jokes.text: May God guide you in your quest.
jokes.setup: David offers Michael a corporate interview
jokes.punchline: Michael responds like he's being sent on a holy mission
jokes.explanation: Michael's tendency to overdramatize simple situations. Good economy but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I suggest that you all go potty now. And then we will be congregating on the par-tay bus.
jokes.setup: Michael organizing the beach trip
jokes.punchline: Talks to grown adults like children with 'potty' and 'par-tay bus'
jokes.explanation: Pure Michael - inappropriate tone for the workplace. Character-driven but predictable.

jokes.text: Oscar, you brought your Speedo, I assume? I don't wear a Speedo, Michael. Well, you can't swim in leather pants.
jokes.setup: Michael assumes Oscar wears a Speedo
jokes.punchline: Oscar corrects him, Michael responds with leather pants comment
jokes.explanation: Relies on gay stereotypes for humor. Not particularly clever or well-structured.

jokes.text: Oh, you know what? Uh, you're not going. It's beach day. Yeah, I know. I'm sorry, Toby.
jokes.setup: Everyone's excited for beach day
jokes.punchline: Michael casually excludes Toby with fake apology
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael-Toby dynamic. The casual cruelty is both funny and uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I want today to be a beautiful memory that the staff and I share after I have passed on to New York. And if Toby is a part of it... then it'll suck.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his beach day vision
jokes.punchline: Reveals the real reason for excluding Toby in the most Michael way possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael logic - turns his petty hatred into something profound. Good structure with the pause.

jokes.text: Hey... want my sunscreen? Oh, great. I forgot mine, and I'm wearing a two-piece. Thanks, Toby.
jokes.setup: Toby offers sunscreen to someone staying behind
jokes.punchline: Meredith mentions wearing a two-piece, creating uncomfortable mental image
jokes.explanation: Meredith's oversharing nature. Visual comedy but relies on 'gross old woman' trope.

jokes.text: I want you to find out about people's character. You know, not their hotness, per se, but their humor and their charisma.
jokes.setup: Michael giving Pam instructions for evaluation
jokes.punchline: Specifies 'not their hotness, per se' revealing what he was initially thinking
jokes.explanation: Michael trying to sound professional while revealing his shallow initial thoughts. Good misdirection.

jokes.text: It's like what happens to a chicken when you take its head away. It dies. Unless you find a new head.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining what happens when you lose a boss
jokes.punchline: Overwrought chicken metaphor that doesn't quite work
jokes.explanation: Michael's tendency to use metaphors that fall apart under scrutiny. The 'new head' addition is absurd.

jokes.text: I need to see which one of these people have the skills to be a chicken head.
jokes.setup: Continuing the chicken metaphor
jokes.punchline: Now evaluating people as potential 'chicken heads'
jokes.explanation: Commits to the absurd metaphor. The phrase 'chicken head' is inherently funny.

jokes.text: You want me to write down people's indefinable qualities?
jokes.setup: Michael wants evaluation of leadership qualities
jokes.punchline: Pam points out the logical impossibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response. Pam cutting through Michael's nonsense with simple logic.

jokes.text: I have the most boring job in the office, so why wouldn't I have the most boring job on beach day?
jokes.setup: Pam gets stuck with note-taking duty
jokes.punchline: Resigned acceptance of her perpetual boring assignments
jokes.explanation: Great character moment. Pam's quiet frustration with her role, delivered with perfect resignation.

jokes.text: I just want to lie on the beach and eat hot dogs. That's all I've ever wanted.
jokes.setup: Beach day is supposed to be fun
jokes.punchline: Stanley's extremely modest life goals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley. The profound simplicity and 'that's all I've ever wanted' elevates it. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, sweet mother of God.
jokes.setup: Michael announces mandatory fun activities
jokes.punchline: Stanley's despairing reaction to learning it's not just beach relaxation
jokes.explanation: Stanley's dreams of simple beach relaxation immediately crushed. Perfect character reaction.

jokes.text: If you don't like it, Stanley, you can go to the back of the bus. Or the front of the bus, or drive the bus.
jokes.setup: Stanley complains about mandatory activities
jokes.punchline: Michael's attempt at bus/civil rights reference that he immediately realizes and tries to backtrack from
jokes.explanation: Michael's accidental racism followed by panicked overcorrection. The cringe builds beautifully.

jokes.text: Funtivities!
jokes.setup: Michael describing mandatory fun activities
jokes.punchline: Creates the portmanteau 'funtivities'
jokes.explanation: Michael's tendency to create unnecessary portmanteaus. Quotable but not brilliant.

jokes.text: Yes, funtivities! I knew it wasn't just a trip to the beach.
jokes.setup: Michael creates 'funtivities'
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately adopts the word and acts like he predicted this
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - immediately latching onto Michael's nonsense and claiming prescience.

jokes.text: Okay, you know what? Your enthusiasm is turning people off.
jokes.setup: Dwight enthusiastically supports Michael's plans
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately turns on his most loyal supporter
jokes.explanation: Michael's classic move of rejecting support when it becomes awkward. Ironic and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Like what? Like everything I said, and everything they did. Just don't... Well, no. Write it down before you forget it. That's... You've just been drawing pictures.
jokes.setup: Michael asks what Pam has written down
jokes.punchline: Reveals Pam has been drawing instead of taking notes
jokes.explanation: Pam's quiet rebellion through art. Michael's frustrated discovery is believable.

jokes.text: Rrrrr-rrrrr! I can't stay mad at you.
jokes.setup: Michael is frustrated with Pam
jokes.punchline: Makes a growling noise then immediately forgives her
jokes.explanation: Michael's inability to stay mad, especially at attractive women. The growl is childish and perfect.

jokes.text: We are situated on the northeast corner of scenic Lake Scranton. America's eight largest indigenous body of water.
jokes.setup: Michael dramatically describing their location
jokes.punchline: Calls it America's 'eight largest' with fake gravitas
jokes.explanation: Michael's tendency to inflate mundane things. 'Eight largest' is oddly specific and unimpressive.

jokes.text: One day, 14 strangers who work together... but only one survivor. What?! Just words... inspiring words.
jokes.setup: Michael builds dramatic tension with Survivor-style narration
jokes.punchline: Stanley's alarmed reaction followed by Michael's weak deflection
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff. Stanley's genuine alarm contrasts with Michael's TV show fantasy.

jokes.text: Each tribe will have a leader that I will pick randomly off the top of my head without thinking. Jim, Dwight, Andy, and Stanley.
jokes.setup: Michael claims random selection
jokes.punchline: Obviously picks the exact people he was evaluating for his job
jokes.explanation: Michael's transparent 'randomness.' The immediate contradiction is well-structured.

jokes.text: Pros: smart, cool, good looking. Remind you of anybody you know? Cons: not a hard worker. I can spend all day on a project, and he will finish the same project in a half an hour.
jokes.setup: Michael evaluating Jim's qualifications
jokes.punchline: Lists Jim's efficiency as a negative trait
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael logic - being too competent is a flaw. The self-aware irony is perfect.

jokes.text: Dwight is an obvious candidate for my job. He has the best sales record in the office. He loves the work. He is, however, an idiot.
jokes.setup: Michael listing Dwight's qualifications
jokes.punchline: Casually adds 'He is, however, an idiot' as a dealbreaker
jokes.explanation: Perfect structure with the matter-of-fact delivery. The build-up makes the punchline land perfectly.

jokes.text: We will be called Gryffindor. Really? Not Slytherin? Slytherin are the bad guys, Jim. I know.
jokes.setup: Dwight picks Harry Potter house name
jokes.punchline: Jim suggests the evil house, knowing Dwight will object
jokes.explanation: Jim poking at Dwight's earnestness. Dwight's serious response to fantasy houses is perfect.

jokes.text: Okay, we will be Voldemort. He who must not be named? I wouldn't do that. Voldemort. Okay, seriously... Voldemort! Voldemort, Voldemort... You really shouldn't be say... Hey, hey, hey. Voldemort. Voldemort! Idiots!
jokes.setup: Jim chooses Voldemort as team name
jokes.punchline: Dwight's increasing panic as Jim keeps saying the name
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with Dwight treating fictional magic rules as real. Jim's psychological warfare at its finest.

jokes.text: I don't care what you call my team. Then I will name your team the red team. No, the blue team.
jokes.setup: Stanley expresses total disinterest
jokes.punchline: Michael arbitrarily changes colors, Stanley still doesn't care
jokes.explanation: Stanley's complete apathy meets Michael's arbitrary authority. Good character contrast.

jokes.text: I am also considering Stanley, because of all the good that black people have done for America.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his management evaluation criteria
jokes.punchline: Awkward racial reasoning that's well-intentioned but completely inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael racial awkwardness. Cringe-worthy but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Andy Bernard. Pros: he's classy, he gets me. He went to Cornell. I trust him. Cons: I don't really trust him.
jokes.setup: Michael evaluating Andy's qualifications
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself about trusting Andy
jokes.explanation: Michael's instant self-contradiction reveals his uncertainty. Good economy and structure.

jokes.text: I am okay if I lose every single contest today. Honestly. Because I see these contests as an opportunity for me to demonstrate what a good sport I am.
jokes.setup: Jim in talking head about the competitions
jokes.punchline: Reveals his strategy is to lose gracefully rather than try to win
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim strategy - gaming the system by being deliberately good-natured. Smart character work.

jokes.text: I don't want to hit the big rock. Don't worry, you're not... I know I'm near the big rock. I just know it. Nowhere near the big rock.
jokes.setup: Phyllis is blindfolded carrying an egg
jokes.punchline: Her increasing paranoia about a rock that may not exist
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with mounting anxiety. The repetition builds the humor effectively.

jokes.text: Come on, andale, arriba, arriba. You have to stop this right now, or I'm not gonna do this anymore.
jokes.setup: Michael encouraging Phyllis with Spanish phrases
jokes.punchline: Phyllis firmly shuts down his inappropriate coaching
jokes.explanation: Michael's casual racism meets Phyllis standing up for herself. The power dynamic shift is satisfying.

jokes.text: Come on, mush! Mush! Come on, you bastard! What the... Damn it, temp!
jokes.setup: Dwight coaching his team member
jokes.punchline: Escalates to calling Ryan 'bastard' and 'temp' with increasing frustration
jokes.explanation: Dwight treating Ryan like a sled dog, then defaulting to 'temp' as the ultimate insult. Perfect character.

jokes.text: How am I supposed to get... Thanks a bunch.
jokes.setup: Michael asks Pam to heat 800 hot dogs in 10 minutes
jokes.punchline: Pam starts to object but Michael walks away, leaving her with impossible task
jokes.explanation: Pam's frustration with Michael's impossible demands. The cut-off protest is relatable.

jokes.text: For those of you who are curious, the world record is 54 1/2 hot dogs. Wow! And you know what? I personally have cooked up enough so that each and every one of you could break that record.
jokes.setup: Michael announces hot dog eating contest with world record
jokes.punchline: Claims to have cooked enough for everyone to break the record simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Michael's math doesn't add up but his enthusiasm is genuine. The logical impossibility is funny.

jokes.text: Can I have a turkey burger? No, I have the only one. I claimed it. Turkey is a healthy meat.
jokes.setup: Oscar asks for healthier option during hot dog contest
jokes.punchline: Michael reveals he has the only healthy option and won't share
jokes.explanation: Michael's petty hoarding of the healthy option while forcing hot dogs on everyone else.

jokes.text: I don't eat meat, Michael. I'm not gonna eat this.
jokes.setup: Hot dog eating contest is announced
jokes.punchline: Kevin, who looks like he loves food, reveals he doesn't eat meat
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectations about Kevin's eating habits. The reveal is surprising but believable.

jokes.text: The winner gets a regional manager's salary for a year, and a Sebring, and the feeling that they are making a difference in the world. Can we just take those first two things?
jokes.setup: Michael reveals the prize includes salary, car, and meaningful work
jokes.punchline: Stanley immediately wants to skip the meaningful work part
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley response. The immediate rejection of 'making a difference' for pure materialism is brilliant.

jokes.text: I would rather work for an upturned broom with a bucket for a head than work for somebody else in this office besides myself.
jokes.setup: Michael announces someone will be his replacement
jokes.punchline: Dwight's bizarre metaphor for preferring any boss other than his coworkers
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd imagery. Only Dwight would come up with 'upturned broom with a bucket for a head.'

jokes.text: And the winner is Andy Bernard, with 14 hot dogs. Team U.S.A.! One came up. 13 hot dogs, everybody.
jokes.setup: Andy wins the hot dog eating contest
jokes.punchline: Immediately throws up one hot dog, reducing his total
jokes.explanation: Classic vomit gag. Functional gross-out humor but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Did you say 'sandwich?' No. I was saying that before. Not now. Now I am saying... Sabotage... ...the ancient Dutch art of screwing up your own team.
jokes.setup: Andy mishears Jim saying something
jokes.punchline: Jim pretends he was explaining 'sabotage' as Dutch art
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jim manipulation. The fake etymology and Andy's confusion create perfect misdirection.

jokes.text: I will misunderstand everything that Andy says, until he goes insane.
jokes.setup: Jim talking to camera after the sabotage exchange
jokes.punchline: Reveals his deliberate strategy to drive Andy crazy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim psychological warfare. The calculated cruelty delivered matter-of-factly is brilliant.

jokes.text: Oh, my God... I have never seen that look in a man's eyes ever. I thought that I might die. On beach day.
jokes.setup: Jim just participated in sumo wrestling with Stanley
jokes.punchline: Describes Stanley's intimidating look and adds 'On beach day' as anticlimax
jokes.explanation: The juxtaposition of mortal fear with 'beach day' is perfect. Jim's genuine terror is funny.

jokes.text: If either of these guys are put in charge of the office, I will transfer to Albany. Gil can come if he wants. I'm kind of looking for a way out of that relationship anyway. I think I might try girls for a while. Angela thinks I can cross over. We'll see.
jokes.setup: Oscar doesn't want Jim or Dwight as boss
jokes.punchline: Escalates from job transfer to relationship issues to sexuality experimentation
jokes.explanation: Unexpected personal revelations triggered by work concerns. Angela as sexuality consultant is absurd.

jokes.text: Go tell somebody. What, Andy? Andy, what should I tell them? Go tell them I'm floating away, obviously! I don't understand what you want from me.
jokes.setup: Andy is floating away and needs help
jokes.punchline: Angela's deliberate obtuseness despite Andy clearly explaining the situation
jokes.explanation: Angela's passive-aggressive non-help is perfectly in character. The repetition builds frustration.

jokes.text: Angela, it's pretty simple. Look at what I'm doing, and go tell somebody it! Sorry. Aah... Bye, Andy. Angela!
jokes.setup: Andy desperately trying to get Angela to help
jokes.punchline: Angela just says goodbye and walks away as he floats away
jokes.explanation: Angela's cruel indifference reaches peak absurdity. The 'Bye, Andy' is devastatingly cold.

jokes.text: At various times, you gave Jim ten points, Dwight a gold star, and Stanley a thumbs up. And I don't really know how to compare those units.
jokes.setup: Michael asks about point totals
jokes.punchline: Pam points out Michael's incompatible scoring system
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pam observation about Michael's arbitrary system. The deadpan delivery is key.

jokes.text: Well, check to see if there's a conversion chart in that notebook. I really doubt it, Michael. Please just check.
jokes.setup: Michael asks about conversion between points, gold stars, and thumbs up
jokes.punchline: Actually expects there might be a conversion chart in a random notebook
jokes.explanation: Michael's magical thinking that solutions will just appear. His insistence despite Pam's logic is perfect.

jokes.text: Yes, I would like to be considered for the corporate position in well. 'In well?' As well. How would that work... in well? I just want to know. That would be fine. This job is in a well. I don't want it.
jokes.setup: Karen tries to say 'as well' during phone interview
jokes.punchline: Jim mishears as 'in well' and builds entire bit around working in a well
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay escalation. Jim's commitment to the bit and Karen's professional call conflict perfectly.

jokes.text: I'd like to try. Pointless. But I'm not kidding. Okay? This is about guts. It takes guts to be a regional manager.
jokes.setup: Pam wants to try the coal walk
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately dismisses her despite his 'courage' speech
jokes.explanation: Michael's sexist dismissal undermines his own premise. The hypocrisy is clear and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Nope. Ju... why not? Come on. Oh, 'cause I don't want my feet to get burned. You do not have what it takes to be a regional manager. That's harsh.
jokes.setup: Michael challenges Jim to walk on coals
jokes.punchline: Jim's matter-of-fact refusal followed by calling Michael's judgment 'harsh'
jokes.explanation: Jim's reasonable response to unreasonable test. The understated 'harsh' deflates Michael perfectly.

jokes.text: My name is Andrew Bernard. I was with a group called Dunder-Mifflin. Hello?
jokes.setup: Andy is floating away on a piece of wood
jokes.punchline: Introduces himself formally to unknown potential rescuers
jokes.explanation: Andy's formal introduction while in distress. The professional courtesy in crisis is absurd.

jokes.text: Because I already did. Remember? I burned my foot on the George Foreman grill.
jokes.setup: Asked why he won't do the coal walk
jokes.punchline: Claims his previous grill accident counts as fire walking
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Michael's previous domestic accident. His logic for equivalence is perfectly Michael.

jokes.text: I will walk and stand on these coals until you award me the position of regional manager! Wow. Dwight. Dwight. Aaah! Ow. Get off there. Get off, get off. Give me the job! I'm not going to give it to you.
jokes.setup: Dwight volunteers to do the coal walk
jokes.punchline: Actually stands on burning coals demanding the job while everyone panics
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight dedication. Only he would actually burn himself for a job. The escalation is both horrifying and hilarious.

jokes.text: Being a boss is also about image. I've never looked like that. That was gross.
jokes.setup: Dwight just burned his feet on coals screaming
jokes.punchline: Michael's shallow takeaway is about looking unprofessional
jokes.explanation: Michael's vanity trumps concern for Dwight's injury. The superficial concern is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Who's Bob Hope? God! He's a, he's a comedian. Oh, like Amanda Bynes. Who's Amanda Bynes? She's from What a Girl Wants. Oh, I love that movie.
jokes.setup: Michael references Bob Hope as comedy standard
jokes.punchline: Kelly compares to Amanda Bynes, creating generational confusion loop
jokes.explanation: Perfect generational comedy with each character missing the others' references. The confusion builds well.

jokes.text: Michael, on Thursday, I'm gonna drive down and interview with David for the open position in New York. Okay, that is not funny. I am deducting 60 points from Voldemort for false pretenses.
jokes.setup: Jim supposed to tell joke in comedy competition
jokes.punchline: Instead reveals he's interviewing for corporate job, Michael treats it as failed joke
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion. Jim uses the comedy contest to deliver serious news, Michael misinterprets completely.

jokes.text: The Aristocrats. A man and his wife and children go into the offices of a talent agency. And the talent agent says, 'Describe your act.' And the man says something really, really raunchy. And the talent representative says, 'What do you call yourselves?' And the man says, 'The Aristocrats.'
jokes.setup: Dwight tells famous comedian's joke
jokes.punchline: Completely sanitizes 'The Aristocrats' by skipping the actual content
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight misunderstanding of famous dirty joke. His earnest clean version completely misses the point.

jokes.text: I did the coal walk. Just, I did it. Michael, you couldn't even do that. Maybe I should be your boss.
jokes.setup: Pam has been quiet and subservient all episode
jokes.punchline: Suddenly asserts herself and challenges Michael's authority
jokes.explanation: Huge character moment for Pam. Her sudden confidence and direct challenge to Michael is shocking and earned.

jokes.text: Why didn't any of you come to my art show? I invited all of you. That really sucked. It's like sometimes some of you act like I don't even exist.
jokes.setup: Pam is in assertive mode
jokes.punchline: Calls out everyone for ignoring her art show invitation
jokes.explanation: Devastating callback to earlier episode. Pam's hurt feelings finally expressed create uncomfortable truth moment.

jokes.text: Jim, I called off my wedding because of you. And now we're not even friends. And things are just, like, weird between us, and that sucks. And I miss you. You were my best friend before you went to Stamford. And I really miss you.
jokes.setup: Pam is being brutally honest
jokes.punchline: Reveals the real reason she called off her wedding and her feelings about Jim
jokes.explanation: Massive character moment. Pam finally admitting the truth about Jim and her wedding is cathartic and perfectly earned.

jokes.text: I shouldn't have been with Roy. There were a lot of reasons to call off my wedding, but the truth is I didn't care about any of those reasons until I met you.
jokes.setup: Pam continuing her honest confession
jokes.punchline: Admits Jim was the catalyst for ending her engagement
jokes.explanation: Peak emotional honesty. This confession is the culmination of seasons of character development and tension.

jokes.text: And now you're with someone else. And that's fine. It's whatever... that's not what I'm... I'm not... Okay, my feet really hurt.
jokes.setup: Pam's emotional confession getting too intense
jokes.punchline: Deflects with mundane complaint about her feet hurting from coal walk
jokes.explanation: Perfect emotional deflection. Pam retreating to physical discomfort when emotions get too heavy is very human.

jokes.text: The thing that I'm just trying to say to you, Jim, and to everyone else in the circle, I guess, is that I miss having fun with you. Just you, not everyone in the circle.
jokes.setup: Pam trying to wrap up her confession
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly clarifies she means just Jim, not the whole group
jokes.explanation: Pam's awkward attempt to be clear about her feelings. The clarification makes it more awkward and honest.

jokes.text: Pam, that was amazing. But I am still looking for someone with a sales background.
jokes.setup: Pam just delivered emotional breakthrough speech
jokes.punchline: Michael completely misses the point and treats it like a job interview
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael response. His complete inability to read the emotional moment is brilliantly tone-deaf and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Wilmaaaaaaaaaa!
jokes.setup: Andy has been floating away all episode
jokes.punchline: Final shot shows him still floating, calling out like Fred Flintstone
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Andy's predicament. The Flintstones reference fits his character and provides closure to the running gag.
